raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "California's oil market is uniquely vulnerable due to its air quality standards and isolated fuels market.",
    "Nearly all in-state oil supply in California comes from a handful of refineries.",
    "A single refinery outage could drastically reduce refining capacity by up to 45% in Northern California and 35% in Southern California.",
    "The higher costs of gasoline in California compared to other states place an undue burden on residents with fixed or limited incomes.",
    "The proposed legislation requires refineries to maintain adequate reserves and properly plan for refinery shutdowns.",
    "The California Energy Commission will have the authority to require petroleum refiners to implement resupply plans that adequately offset production losses from refinery maintenance.",
    "The Commission can only adopt regulations if it determines they will lead to lower average retail prices, increase the fuel supply, and reduce price volatility at the pumps."
  ]
}
```
